zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

archetype_rationale: Differs from wealth.com's 'multi-tenant-AI-fintech-SaaS' archetype on three axes: (1) single-user not multi-tenant, (2) desktop/CLI not browser SaaS, (3) different IRS code section (§1 income, not §2001 estate). Shares: regulated workload, deterministic-calc requirement, citation grounding for any AI extraction, audit-log requirement. The shared bits are factory-reusable; the divergent bits are product-specific tailoring.

surface_flag_rationale_vs_wealthcom.ui_surface: WEALTH.COM=true (Heritage Map, Scenario Builder UI). HERE=false (CLI/library only). Demonstrates factory tailors UI gates per surface.
surface_flag_rationale_vs_wealthcom.api_surface: WEALTH.COM=true (REST API for advisor workbench). HERE=false (single-user tool, no API). Demonstrates API contract gates only fire when api_surface=true.
surface_flag_rationale_vs_wealthcom.scenario_driven: Both true. Shared. (Income tax is a deterministic scenario; estate tax + GRAT is a deterministic scenario.)

risk_profile_rationale_vs_wealthcom.safety_high: Both: tax-calc errors carry IRS exposure. Shared. Deterministic-calc separation transfers.
risk_profile_rationale_vs_wealthcom.privacy_medium_vs_high: Wealth.com=high (multi-tenant, PHI in HCDs). Here=medium (single-user, no PHI, no AHCD documents). The privacy-heavy tailoring (per-tenant KMS, three-layer isolation) is NOT applicable.
risk_profile_rationale_vs_wealthcom.security_low_vs_high: Wealth.com=high (multi-tenant SaaS, valuable target). Here=low (single-user desktop tool, no SaaS surface). Defense-in-depth is appropriate level for risk; 3-layer tenant isolation would be theatre here.
risk_profile_rationale_vs_wealthcom.compliance_high: Both: IRS rules + (here) AICPA professional standards for CPAs. Shared.
risk_profile_rationale_vs_wealthcom.scale_low_vs_medium: Wealth.com=medium at v0 (multi-tenant evolution). Here=low (single user, single machine).

rup.reason: Single-developer narrow scope; phase discipline overhead exceeds value

mda.reason: No platform-independent vs platform-specific separation needed at this scale

ddd.reason: Single bounded context (TaxFiling); ubiquitous-language overhead exceeds value

tdd_bdd.reason: Same as wealth.com run — golden-dataset TDD for deterministic calc rules. SHARED PATTERN.

methodology_blend_divergence_note: The wealth.com tailoring blended 6 methods (RUP+MDA+DDD+TDD+SRE+SSDF). The TaxBaseline Mini tailoring blends 1 (TDD/BDD only). This is the factory tailoring per archetype, not blanket-applying a 'serious project blend'. If a future review of the factory's methodology-blender suggests it always blends 6 methods regardless of risk profile, that suggestion is itself a slop signal — caught here.
mandatory_testing_classes_divergence_not: Wealth.com mandated 11 testing classes. TaxBaseline Mini mandates 3. The factory tailors testing rigor to risk profile + surface flags, not blanket-applies. integration / contract_api / browser_wysiwyg / visual_regression / production_drill / accessibility_ux are all NOT applicable here.
mandatory_artifact_ids: ADR-T-002 (no-multi-tenant-by-design, deliberately NOT transferred from ADR-002)
pytest test file with parametrized golden cases
Adversarial review record confirming no wealth.com vocabulary leakage
human_boundary_triggers: Any addition of multi-tenant or multi-user concepts (would change archetype)
Any addition of estate/gift/GST tax (would replicate wealth.com surface)
anti_overfit_rules_for_factory: Forbidden tokens in this run's tree (CI grep): Household, Trust, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, CLAT, Dynasty, OBBBA, Ester, Sage, Heritage Map, Family Office, EstateFlow, EstateCompass, distribution_rule. Allowed: 'beneficiary' in tax-form context (1099 beneficiary designations) — but flag for review.
ADR-T-001 must explicitly cite ADR-001 as the parent pattern; the *pattern* transfers, the *content* (estate tax → income tax) does not.
ADR-T-002 must explicitly cite ADR-002 and document why it is NOT transferred (different archetype: single-user not multi-tenant).
If any factory-reusable rubric or template *requires* fields that are wealth.com-specific (e.g., 'tenant_id', 'household_id'), that is a factory-overfit signal — surface it to df-feedback-learning.
